title: PMP Formulas
summary: The formula bank — earned value, forecasting, three-point estimates, communication channels and EMV, with what each result means.

sections.heading: Earned value — the three measures
sections.body:
Every earned value calculation is built from three dollar figures measured at the same point in time, against the total Budget at Completion (BAC):

- Planned Value (PV) — the budgeted cost of the work that should be done by now.
- Earned Value (EV) — the budgeted cost of the work actually done. EV = % complete x BAC.
- Actual Cost (AC) — what has actually been spent to do that work.

Get these three straight and the rest follows: variances are differences, indices are ratios, and forecasts project them to the end.
sections.tip: EV is the hinge of every formula. EV = percent complete x BAC — always the VALUE earned, never the money spent.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 320 190' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><line x1='40' y1='20' x2='40' y2='160' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='40' y1='160' x2='304' y2='160' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='210' y1='28' x2='210' y2='160' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><text x='210' y='175' fill='currentColor' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>time now</text><path d='M44,156 C112,150 168,92 300,44' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.6' stroke-dasharray='5 4'/><path d='M44,156 C100,150 162,120 210,106' stroke='#2F6F5E' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M44,156 C100,147 158,108 210,90' stroke='#B5462F' stroke-width='2'/><text x='276' y='44' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='10'>PV</text><text x='216' y='108' fill='#2F6F5E' font-size='10'>EV</text><text x='216' y='90' fill='#B5462F' font-size='10'>AC</text></svg>
figure.caption: PV planned, EV earned, AC actual. Here EV sits below PV (behind) and AC above EV (over budget).

sections.heading: Variances & indices
sections.body:
Variances are differences (EV minus the other measure); a negative variance is bad. Indices are ratios (EV over the other measure); below 1.0 is bad.

- Cost Variance: CV = EV - AC. Negative = over budget.
- Schedule Variance: SV = EV - PV. Negative = behind schedule.
- Cost Performance Index: CPI = EV / AC. Below 1.0 = over budget (getting less than a pound of value per pound spent).
- Schedule Performance Index: SPI = EV / PV. Below 1.0 = behind schedule.
sections.tip: EV comes first in every one. Variance negative = bad; index below 1.0 = bad. CV/CPI use AC; SV/SPI use PV.

sections.heading: Forecasting to completion
sections.body:
Forecasts project today's performance forward. Pick the EAC formula that matches the question's assumption:

- Estimate at Completion (EAC), if current variances are TYPICAL: EAC = BAC / CPI.
- EAC, if the overrun was a ONE-OFF (atypical): EAC = AC + (BAC - EV).
- EAC, if BOTH cost and schedule will persist: EAC = AC + (BAC - EV) / (CPI x SPI).
- Estimate to Complete: ETC = EAC - AC (what's left to spend).
- Variance at Completion: VAC = BAC - EAC (projected over/under at the end; negative = over budget).
- To-Complete Performance Index: TCPI = (BAC - EV) / (BAC - AC) to still hit the BAC, or (BAC - EV) / (EAC - AC) to hit a new EAC.
sections.tip: TCPI above 1.0 means you must work MORE efficiently than planned to make the target — usually a warning sign.

sections.heading: Three-point (PERT) estimates
sections.body:
Three-point estimating replaces a single guess with optimistic (O), most likely (M) and pessimistic (P) values:

- Triangular (simple average): E = (O + M + P) / 3.
- Beta / PERT (weights the most likely): E = (O + 4M + P) / 6.
- Standard deviation of the estimate: SD = (P - O) / 6.
- Variance = SD squared.

The beta version is the classic PMP formula because the 4x weight on Most Likely makes it a weighted average. A wider O-to-P spread means a larger SD and more risk.
sections.tip: Beta = (O + 4M + P) / 6 with the 4 on Most Likely. Triangular just divides by 3 with no weighting.

sections.heading: Communication channels & EMV
sections.body:
Two quick formulas worth memorising:

- Communication channels: n(n - 1) / 2, where n is the number of people. Adding one person adds n new channels, so channels grow fast. Watch whether the question includes the project manager in n.
- Expected Monetary Value: EMV = probability x impact, in money. Threats are negative, opportunities positive. Decision trees compare the EMV of each branch and pick the best.

One contract figure sometimes appears: the Point of Total Assumption (PTA) for an FPIF contract = ((Ceiling Price - Target Price) / Buyer's share ratio) + Target Cost — the cost above which the seller bears all further overrun.
sections.tip: Channels: 10 people = 10 x 9 / 2 = 45. If a question adds people, compute before and after and take the difference.

source: PM Cram study notes — reworded for the current ECO-based PMP exam.
